title: Version SHAs: Data SHA vs. Metadata SHA
text: data SHA hashes parquet representation, -memory data frame. Concretely (R/utils-sha.R): properties gives us: Cross-language stability. language produces parquet bytes produces SHA. makes datom usable R today Python tomorrow without coordination. Column row order matter default. reordered data frame different table. sort_columns sort_rows arguments callers want order-independent identity, default “wrote get.” Empty data frames rejected. Computing SHA zero rows zero columns almost always bug; datom errors instead.

title: Version SHAs: Data SHA vs. Metadata SHA
text: metadata SHA careful. satisfy three properties don’t fall automatically: JSON round-trip stability. reader pulling metadata S3 gets JSON-parsed list, original R object. R’s serialize() type-sensitive (10L 10 serialize differently); JSON-parsed data drops distinction. hashed R object directly, metadata different SHAs -memory vs. round-tripped. Field-order independence. Two writers building metadata different orders produce SHA. Volatile-field exclusion. Two writes identical content different times must hash value, version dedup works. implementation handles three: serialize = FALSE load-bearing detail. tells digest() hash bytes JSON string, R-serialized representation. ’s makes SHA portable across git -> S3 -> reader path.
code: .datom_compute_metadata_sha <- function(metadata) {   # 1. Strip volatile fields that don't define content identity   volatile <- c("created_at", "datom_version")   semantic <- metadata[setdiff(names(metadata), volatile)]    # 2. Sort field names for order independence   sorted_metadata <- semantic[sort(names(semantic))]    # 3. Hash the JSON canonical form, not the R object   canonical <- jsonlite::toJSON(sorted_metadata, auto_unbox = TRUE)   digest::digest(canonical, algo = "sha256", serialize = FALSE) }

title: Copilot Instructions for datom
text: datom R package version-controlled data management. stores tabular data S3 git-tracked metadata, enabling reproducibility clinical/scientific workflows. Pre-release status: package released production data products depend . Proceed without backward compatibility lifecycle management concerns — rename freely, delete freely, break APIs needed. Core concept: Tables abstracted code git; actual data cloud storage (parquet format). Git + GitHub remote mandatory, always (decision reaffirmed Phase 16, 2026-04-29). datom git-tracked metadata system; “local-/ -remote” mode one. local filesystem backend (datom_store_local()) controls parquet bytes live — metadata still requires data_repo_url gov_repo_url (today: GitHub). vignette, README, onboarding flow implies otherwise wrong. Single-user -GitHub demos explicitly rejected scope; readers can’t get GITHUB_PAT audience. future phase wants add remoteless mode, must update line first.

title: datom (development version)
text: New functions: datom_summary(conn) – compact, role-aware overview single project (name, role, backend/root/prefix, table count, total versions, last write, – developers – data git remote URL). Returns S3 datom_summary object print method. Reads .metadata/manifest.json. datom_projects(x) – lists every project registered shared governance repo. Accepts either datom_conn (uses local gov clone present – offline, fast) datom_store (lets caller enumerate portfolio connecting one project). Returns sorted data frame: name, data_backend, data_root, data_prefix, registered_at. Corrupt registry entries warn skipped. Schema additions (pre-release; migration concern): ref.json: current$type previous[].type now record data backend ("s3" "local"). Required readers can identify backend without already holding store. Internal: New storage list dispatch: .datom_storage_list_objects(conn, prefix) .datom_s3_list_objects() existing .datom_local_list_objects() arms. .datom_gov_list_projects(gov_conn, gov_local_path) – prefers local gov clone; falls back storage walk. Pure read; GOV_SEAM helper.

title: datom (development version)
text: Breaking changes: datom_store(): argument remote_url renamed data_repo_url. New arguments gov_repo_url gov_local_path shared governance repository. project.yaml: repos block now two children, repos.data repos.governance (remote_url; latter also local_path). dispatch.json, ref.json, migration_history.json longer live data repo’s .datom/ directory. live governance repo projects/{project_name}/{file}.json mirrored governance storage path. New functions: datom_init_gov() — one-time bootstrap shared governance repository (one per organization / governance bucket). datom_decommission() — tear project (data storage + GitHub repo + governance entry). Requires confirm = "{project_name}" literal match. datom_pull_gov() — pull governance repo (rare; mostly diagnostics). Changed behavior: datom_clone() now clones data repo governance repo (sibling default; reuses existing gov clone matches gov_repo_url). datom_pull() now pulls repos default. datom_sync_dispatch() now produces governance-repo commit (storage upload) projects/{project_name}/dispatch.json. data repo untouched. datom_init_repo() now requires gov_repo_url store writes projects/{project_name}/{ref,dispatch,migration_history}.json governance repo + storage separate commit data repo’s initial commit. Two distinct commits across two histories. Connection objects gain gov_local_path field. Developer connections read ref.json local gov clone; reader connections read gov storage. write-time guard always reads storage detect stale clones.

text: Pre-release. API experimental may change without notice.
